2605.18556.title: Key-Gram: Extensible World Knowledge for Embodied Manipulation
2605.18556.paper_summary: Embodied control increasingly requires models to follow compositional language instructions while reasoning over dynamic visual states. However, current vision-language-action policies and world-action models often couple linguistic knowledge with visual computation in a shared backbone or conditioning pathway, leading to modality competition and making knowledge extension dependent on backbone updates. In this paper, we introduce Key-Gram, a conditional-memory framework that separates language-derived world knowledge from visual-state reasoning for embodied control. At its core is a memory module that decomposes an instruction into task-specific key-grams, retrieves static linguistic priors through deterministic hashed lookup, and injects the retrieved entries into selected hidden layers through context-aware gating and lightweight convolutional fusion. This design allows the backbone to devote its main capacity to visual reasoning and action inference, while reusable instruction knowledge is stored in an extensible external memory. The logical memory table can be conveniently partitioned during training and, due to its $O(1)$ lookup pattern, efficiently placed on host memory during inference. Across RoboTwin2.0, LIBERO/LIBERO-Plus, and real-world dual-arm manipulation, Key-Gram consistently improves both $π_{0}$ and $π_{0.5}$ backbones, with average relative gains of $29.5\%/9.9\%$ on RoboTwin2.0, $35.8\%/4.5\%$ on LIBERO-Plus transfer without target-domain fine-tuning, and $15.4\%/8.1\%$ on real-world long-horizon tasks. These results demonstrate that externalized linguistic memory provides an effective and extensible mechanism for improving compositional grounding, transfer, and real-world manipulation.
2605.18556.paper_summary_zh: 具身控制越來越需要模型在推理動態視覺狀態的同時，遵循組合語言指令。<lb/>然而，當前的視覺-語言-行動政策和世界-行動模型通常將語言知識與視覺計算結合在一個共享的骨幹或條件路徑中，導致模態競爭，並使知識擴展依賴於骨幹更新。<lb/>在本文中，我們介紹了Key-Gram，一個條件記憶框架，將語言衍生的世界知識與視覺狀態推理分離，以實現具身控制。<lb/>其核心是一個記憶模塊，將指令分解為任務特定的關鍵語素，通過確定性哈希查找檢索靜態語言先驗，並通過上下文感知的閘控和輕量級卷積融合將檢索到的條目注入選定的隱藏層。<lb/>這種設計使得骨幹可以將其主要能力專注於視覺推理和行動推斷，而可重用的指令知識則存儲在可擴展的外部記憶中。<lb/>邏輯記憶表在訓練過程中可以方便地進行分區，並且由於其$O(1)$查找模式，在推理過程中可以高效地放置在主機記憶中。<lb/>在RoboTwin2.0、LIBERO/LIBERO-Plus和現實世界的雙臂操作中，Key-Gram始終改善了$π_{0}$和$π_{0.5}$骨幹，RoboTwin2.0的平均相對增益為$29.5\%/9.9\%$，LIBERO-Plus轉移的平均相對增益為$35.8\%/4.5\%$，而在現實世界的長期任務中為$15.4\%/8.1\%$。<lb/>這些結果表明，外部化的語言記憶提供了一種有效且可擴展的機制，用於改善組合基礎、轉移和現實世界的操作。
2605.18556.author: Jingjing Fan et.al.
2605.18556.authors: Jingjing Fan, Siyuan Li, Botao Ren, Zhidong Deng
